title: Cheesy Dreams Come True Day
story: Millions of people grab their forks and dive into bowls of creamy, cheesy mac and cheese on July 14 for National Mac and Cheese Day! This beloved comfort food gets its moment to shine as families make homemade versions with fancy cheeses, while others heat up their favorite boxed brands. Restaurants create special mac and cheese dishes with wild toppings like bacon, lobster, or jalapeños. Social media fills with photos of golden, gooey masterpieces. Some people even host mac and cheese cook-offs to see who makes the cheesiest, most delicious bowl of this ultimate comfort food.
activity_question: What do some people host on National Mac and Cheese Day?
bonus_fact: Americans eat about 2 billion boxes of mac and cheese every year!

title: Shark Awareness Day: Fin-tastic Facts!
story: Sharks get their moment to shine every July 14 on Shark Awareness Day! People around the world celebrate these amazing ocean predators by sharing cool shark facts on social media, visiting aquariums, and watching shark documentaries. Marine biologists use this day to teach everyone that sharks aren't scary monsters—they're actually super important for keeping our oceans healthy. Many aquariums host special shark talks and feeding shows, while conservation groups raise money to protect shark habitats. Some people even go shark cage diving or snorkeling to see these incredible creatures up close safely!
activity_question: What do marine biologists teach people about sharks on Shark Awareness Day?
activity_choices: That sharks are important for healthy oceans
activity_answer: That sharks are important for healthy oceans
bonus_fact: Sharks have been around for over 400 million years—longer than trees!

title: Celebrating Every Color of the Rainbow
story: July 14 is International Non-Binary People's Day, when communities around the world celebrate people who don't identify as just male or female. Friends and families show support by sharing colorful posts on social media, wearing yellow, white, purple, and black colors, and learning about different ways people express themselves. Schools and libraries often host special events where kids can explore books about identity and friendship. It's a day that reminds everyone that people are wonderfully different, and that's something worth celebrating together.
activity_question: What colors do people often wear to show support on International Non-Binary People's Day?
follow_up_question: How do you think celebrating differences helps make communities stronger and more welcoming?
